text.title: Schedule Development Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Padding individual activity durations instead of using project-end and feeding buffers.
antipattern.root-cause: Per-task padding hides slack, triggers Parkinson's Law, makes the critical path invisible.
antipattern.fix: Apply Critical Chain: one project buffer at end (50% of CP σ-sum), feeding buffers at merge points.

antipattern.symptom: Reporting only 'the critical path' as a single chain when multiple paths tie.
antipattern.root-cause: Multi-path criticality means all paths with float ≤ 0 must be managed.
antipattern.fix: Print all paths with float ≤ 0; not 'the' path.

antipattern.symptom: LLM produces wildly optimistic durations because training data emphasises happy paths.
antipattern.root-cause: Heavy-tailed distribution: P50 schedule slips ~70% of the time.
antipattern.fix: Force three-point estimation with explicit pessimistic values from the team; reject point estimates for uncertain activities.
